docs.title: pytest-r-snapshot
docs.text:
A pytest plugin for snapshot testing against reference outputs produced by R code. Particularly useful for test-driven development (TDD) when porting R packages to Python.

It is designed for a portable workflow:

  • Record locally (requires R): run labelled R chunks embedded in your Python tests and write snapshot files.
  • Replay everywhere (default; no R required): read committed snapshot files and compare them to Python outputs.

The R chunks live close to the test assertion (copy/paste-able from R scripts or R Markdown), but snapshots are stored as deterministic UTF-8 text files.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
You can install pytest-r-snapshot from PyPI:

pip install pytest-r-snapshot

If your project is managed with uv, add it as a dev dependency:

uv add --dev pytest-r-snapshot

Or install the development version from GitHub:

git clone https://github.com/nanxstats/pytest-r-snapshot.git
cd pytest-r-snapshot
python3 -m pip install -e .

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
Embed a labelled R fenced chunk (commented or in a docstring), then compare your Python output to the recorded snapshot:

def test_summary_matches_r(r_snapshot):
    # ```{r, summary}
    # x <- c(1, 2, 3)
    # summary(x)
    # ```

    actual = my_python_summary(...)
    r_snapshot.assert_match_text(actual, name="summary")

Generate (or update) snapshots locally:

pytest --r-snapshot=record

Commit the generated snapshot files, and run CI with the default replay mode (no R required).

docs.title: What you get
docs.text:
• A pytest fixture r_snapshot with methods:
    • assert_match_text(actual, name=..., ext=".txt", normalize=...)
    • read_text(name=..., ext=".txt")
    • record_text(name=..., ext=".txt")
    • path_for(name=..., ext=".txt")
  • Snapshot modes: replay (default), record, auto.
  • Chunk extraction for R Markdown-style fenced chunks inside Python source:
    • Commented fences (chunk lines prefixed with #).
    • Raw chunks in docstrings / multi-line strings.
  • Configurable R execution context: Rscript path, environment, working directory, timeout, encoding.

docs.title: New features
docs.text:
• Initial release of the pytest-r-snapshot pytest plugin for snapshot testing against R code outputs.
  • Extract labelled R fenced chunks from Python source (commented chunks and raw chunks in docstrings/multiline strings).
  • Snapshot modes: replay (default), record, and auto.
  • Default snapshot layout: __r_snapshots__/<test_file_stem>/<name><ext> with configurable root via --r-snapshot-dir / r_snapshot_dir.
  • r_snapshot fixture with assert_match_text(), read_text(), record_text(), and path_for().
  • Configurable R execution: Rscript path, env overrides, working directory, timeout, and snapshot encoding.
  • Optional marker @pytest.mark.r_snapshot(...) and decorator alias pytest_r_snapshot.r_snapshot(...).
  • Built-in text normalizers: newline normalization and stripping trailing whitespace.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text: • Added README and mkdocs articles (setup, usage, configuration, troubleshooting, design, migration).

docs.title: Testing
docs.text: • Added a pytester-based test suite with a mock R runner (no real R required).

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: This page lists configuration options for pytest-r-snapshot and how they are resolved.

docs.title: Snapshot mode
docs.text:
Controls whether snapshots are read from disk or recorded by running R:

  • CLI: --r-snapshot={replay,record,auto}
    • Aliases: --r-snapshot-record, --r-snapshot-auto
  • Ini: r_snapshot_mode
  • Default: replay

docs.title: Snapshot directory
docs.text:
Controls where snapshot files are stored:

  • CLI: --r-snapshot-dir=PATH
  • Ini: r_snapshot_dir
  • Default: <test_dir>/__r_snapshots__/<test_file_stem>/...

When r_snapshot_dir is set, snapshots are written under:

<r_snapshot_dir>/<test_file_stem>/<name><ext>

docs.title: R execution options
docs.text: These are only used when recording is required (record mode, or auto mode with a missing snapshot).

docs.title: Rscript executable
docs.text:
• CLI: --r-snapshot-rscript=PATH
  • Ini: r_snapshot_rscript
  • Default: Rscript

docs.title: Working directory
docs.text:
• CLI: --r-snapshot-cwd=PATH
  • Ini: r_snapshot_cwd
  • Default: pytest root directory

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
• CLI: --r-snapshot-env=KEY=VALUE (repeatable)
  • Ini: r_snapshot_env (a list of KEY=VALUE lines)
  • Default: no overrides (inherits the current environment)

Example:

pytest --r-snapshot=record --r-snapshot-env=R_LIBS_USER=/path/to/rlibs

docs.title: Timeout
docs.text:
• CLI: --r-snapshot-timeout=SECONDS
  • Ini: r_snapshot_timeout
  • Default: no timeout

docs.title: Snapshot encoding
docs.text:
• CLI: --r-snapshot-encoding=ENC
  • Ini: r_snapshot_encoding
  • Default: utf-8

docs.title: Configuration precedence
docs.text:
Settings are resolved in this order (highest wins):

  1. CLI options
  2. r_snapshot_settings fixture (if you override it in conftest.py)
  3. pyproject.toml / ini configuration
  4. Built-in defaults

docs.title: conftest.py hook (advanced)
docs.text:
You can override the session-scoped r_snapshot_settings fixture to compute defaults dynamically:

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from pytest_r_snapshot import RSnapshotSettings, SnapshotMode


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def r_snapshot_settings() -> RSnapshotSettings:
    return RSnapshotSettings(
        mode=SnapshotMode.REPLAY,
        snapshot_dir=Path("tests/fixtures/r_outputs"),
        rscript="Rscript",
        env={"R_LIBS_USER": "/path/to/rlibs"},
        timeout=30,
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

CLI flags still override this fixture.

docs.title: Design
docs.text: pytest-r-snapshot is intentionally small and modular. The public API is the r_snapshot fixture (an RSnapshot instance) and a few helpers; everything else is internal plumbing to keep parsing, subprocess execution, and snapshot I/O isolated and testable.

docs.title: High-level flow
docs.text:
When a test calls r_snapshot.assert_match_text(...):

  1. The snapshot file path is resolved for (test_file, name, ext).
  2. The expected value is obtained based on the configured mode:
    • replay: read the snapshot file
    • record: run R and rewrite the snapshot file
    • auto: record only if the snapshot file is missing
  3. Expected and actual are newline-normalized (and optionally user-normalized).
  4. If they differ, pytest-friendly unified diff output is included in the failure message.

docs.title: Module responsibilities
docs.text:
The implementation follows a "thin orchestrator" pattern:

  • pytest_r_snapshot/chunks.py
    • Parses labelled R fenced chunks from Python source files.
    • Scans line-by-line and tracks start_line/end_line for diagnostics.
    • Supports both commented chunks and raw chunks in docstrings/multiline strings.
  • pytest_r_snapshot/runner.py
    • Defines the RRunner protocol and a subprocess implementation.
    • Runs Rscript --vanilla <tempfile.R> and returns captured stdout.
    • Wraps user code in a minimal R template using capture.output({ ... }) for deterministic text snapshots.
  • pytest_r_snapshot/snapshot.py
    • Implements the public RSnapshot class.
    • Resolves snapshot paths, reads/writes snapshot files, and performs comparisons.
    • Delegates parsing to chunks and execution to an RRunner.
  • pytest_r_snapshot/settings.py
    • Defines settings (RSnapshotSettings) and SnapshotMode, plus parsing helpers.
  • pytest_r_snapshot/plugin.py
    • Integrates with pytest: CLI/ini options, marker registration, and fixtures.
    • Builds effective settings using the precedence model: CLI overrides everything; a conftest.py settings fixture can override ini defaults.
  • pytest_r_snapshot/errors.py
    • Custom exception types for clear, targeted error messages.
  • pytest_r_snapshot/normalize.py
    • Small, generic normalization helpers (newline normalization, trimming).

docs.title: Configuration precedence
docs.text:
Settings are merged in this order:

  1. Built-in defaults
  2. pyproject.toml / pytest ini values
  3. A user-provided r_snapshot_settings fixture (session-scoped) in conftest.py
  4. CLI options (highest precedence)

This allows project-wide defaults to live in version control while still letting developers override behavior locally via CLI.

docs.title: Testing strategy
docs.text:
The plugin's own tests are hermetic and do not require R:

  • Chunk parsing and path resolution are unit-tested directly.
  • Mode behavior is tested using pytester and a fake RRunner provided by overriding the r_snapshot_runner session fixture.
  • A small contract test verifies that the subprocess runner raises a helpful error when Rscript is missing.

This keeps CI fast and avoids coupling plugin correctness to a specific R installation.

docs.title: Migration
docs.text: This guide outlines how to migrate from an ad hoc "run R chunks and save outputs" approach to pytest-r-snapshot.

docs.title: Keep your existing R chunks
docs.text:
If your tests already include commented fenced chunks like:

# ```{r, minimal_rtf}
# ...
# ```

you can keep them as-is. The plugin uses a compatible chunk syntax and requires only that every chunk has a unique label per file.

docs.title: Replace manual readers with the fixture
docs.text:
Instead of custom "expected output" readers, use:

  • r_snapshot.read_text(name="label", ext=".rtf") to load an expected snapshot
  • r_snapshot.assert_match_text(actual, name="label", ext=".rtf") to compare with diffs

docs.title: Match your existing snapshot directory
docs.text:
If you already store recorded outputs under a specific root, configure the plugin:

pytest --r-snapshot=record --r-snapshot-dir=vendor/rtflite/tests/fixtures/r_outputs

With --r-snapshot-dir=..., the plugin writes snapshots under:

vendor/rtflite/tests/fixtures/r_outputs/<test_file_stem>/<name><ext>

If your existing layout differs, the simplest migration is to record once with the plugin and commit the new layout.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text: This guide shows how to set up a project to use pytest-r-snapshot, including a recommended CI workflow that does not require R.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python >= 3.10
  • pytest (installed automatically as a dependency)
  • R (optional): Required for recording snapshots (--r-snapshot=record or --r-snapshot=auto when a snapshot is missing). Not required for replaying snapshots (the default mode)

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install pytest-r-snapshot

uv add --dev pytest-r-snapshot

Pytest will discover the plugin automatically when it is installed.

docs.title: Configure pytest (optional)
docs.text:
All options can be set via command line flags, or in pyproject.toml under [tool.pytest.ini_options].

Example configuration:

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
r_snapshot_mode = "replay"                       # replay|record|auto
r_snapshot_dir = "tests/__r_snapshots__"         # optional custom root
r_snapshot_rscript = "Rscript"                   # or an absolute path
r_snapshot_cwd = "."                             # default: pytest root
r_snapshot_env = ["R_LIBS_USER=/path/to/rlibs"]  # optional, repeatable
r_snapshot_timeout = "30"                        # optional seconds
r_snapshot_encoding = "utf-8"

Notes:

If r_snapshot_dir is set, snapshots are written under:

<r_snapshot_dir>/<test_file_stem>/<name><ext>

If r_snapshot_dir is not set, snapshots are written next to the test file:

<test_dir>/__r_snapshots__/<test_file_stem>/<name><ext>

docs.title: Recording workflow
docs.text:
1. Write tests that embed labelled R chunks (see docs/articles/usage.md).
  2. Record snapshots locally: 
    pytest --r-snapshot=record
  3. Commit the snapshot files to your repository.
  4. Run CI in replay mode (default), without requiring R.

auto mode is useful for bootstrapping snapshots:

pytest --r-snapshot=auto

docs.title: GitHub Actions (CI without R)
docs.text:
The recommended workflow is to run tests in replay mode, relying only on committed snapshots.

name: tests

on:
  push:
  pull_request:

jobs:
  pytest:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.14"
          cache: pip

      - name: Install
        run: |
          python -m pip install -U pip
          python -m pip install -e ".[test]"

      - name: Run tests (replay mode, default)
        run: |
          pytest -q

If your project does not define extras, replace the install step with your preferred dependency installer (for example, pip install -r requirements.txt).

docs.title: CI that records snapshots (optional)
docs.text:
Snapshot recording changes files on disk and is typically done locally and committed.

If you want a manual workflow that can regenerate snapshots in CI, you can add a separate job that installs R and runs pytest --r-snapshot=record, then uploads the generated snapshots as an artifact for review.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: This page covers the most common failures and how to fix them.

docs.title: Snapshot file is missing
docs.text:
You are in replay mode (default) and the snapshot file does not exist.

Fix:

  • Record snapshots locally and commit them:

    pytest --r-snapshot=record

  • Double-check name= and ext=... match the recorded snapshot filename.

  • If you configured r_snapshot_dir, confirm the snapshot root is correct.

docs.title: R chunk '...' not found
docs.text:
The snapshot name you requested does not match any labelled R chunk in the current test file.

Fix:

  • Ensure the chunk is labelled and the label matches name=....
  • If you use @pytest.mark.r_snapshot("name"), the plugin will fail early with a list of available labels.

docs.title: Missing R chunk label
docs.text:
Every fenced R chunk must have a label.

Accepted examples:

```{r, my_label}
...
```

```{r label}
...
```

Fix: Add a label to the chunk header.

docs.title: Duplicate R chunk label
docs.text:
Labels must be unique per Python file.

Fix: Rename one of the conflicting chunks.

docs.title: Rscript executable not found
docs.text:
Recording requires Rscript to be available.

Fix:

  • Install R, or configure the Rscript path:

    pytest --r-snapshot=record --r-snapshot-rscript=/path/to/Rscript

Or set r_snapshot_rscript in pyproject.toml.

docs.title: R execution failed
docs.text:
The R process returned a non-zero exit code. The failure message includes stdout/stderr.

Fix:

  • Run in record mode locally to reproduce and inspect the full error:

    pytest --r-snapshot=record -q

  • Verify required R packages are installed and discoverable (for example, set R_LIBS_USER via --r-snapshot-env).

docs.title: Encoding issues
docs.text:
Snapshots are stored as UTF-8 by default.

Fix: If you must use a different encoding, set r_snapshot_encoding / --r-snapshot-encoding.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: pytest-r-snapshot records reference outputs from labelled R code chunks embedded in your Python test files, then compares your Python outputs to those recorded snapshots.

docs.title: Basic fixture usage
docs.text:
Use the r_snapshot fixture and compare text output:

def test_summary_matches_r(r_snapshot):
    # ```{r, summary}
    # x <- c(1, 2, 3)
    # summary(x)
    # ```

    actual = my_python_summary(...)
    r_snapshot.assert_match_text(actual, name="summary")

Record snapshots locally:

pytest --r-snapshot=record

By default, CI can run pytest in replay mode without R.

docs.title: Commented chunks
docs.text:
Commented chunks are convenient when you want the R code to live right above the assertion:

def test_x(r_snapshot):
    # ```{r, label}
    # x <- 1 + 1
    # print(x)
    # ```
    r_snapshot.assert_match_text("2", name="label")

Rules:

  • Start/end fences must be a standalone line containing three backticks.
  • Each body line must start with # (an optional single space after # is allowed).
  • Every chunk must have a label, and labels must be unique per file.

docs.title: Docstring / multiline string chunks
docs.text:
You can also place raw fenced chunks inside a docstring or multiline string:

def test_summary_matches_r(r_snapshot):
    """
    ```{r, summary}
    x <- c(1, 2, 3)
    summary(x)
    ```
    """
    r_snapshot.assert_match_text(my_python_summary(...), name="summary")

The plugin dedents the chunk body to remove Python indentation while keeping relative indentation.

docs.title: Snapshot file layout
docs.text:
By default, for tests/test_example.py, snapshots are stored under:

tests/__r_snapshots__/test_example/<name><ext>

You can override the snapshot root directory with --r-snapshot-dir / r_snapshot_dir. In that case snapshots are stored under:

<r_snapshot_dir>/test_example/<name><ext>

The default file extension is .txt. You can change it per assertion:

r_snapshot.assert_match_text(actual, name="minimal_rtf", ext=".rtf")

Both "rtf" and ".rtf" are accepted.

docs.title: Snapshot modes
docs.text:
• replay (default): never runs R; fails if the snapshot is missing.
  • record: always runs R and overwrites snapshots.
  • auto: runs R only when a snapshot is missing.

Examples:

pytest                  # replay
pytest --r-snapshot=auto
pytest --r-snapshot=record

docs.title: R execution configuration (when recording)
docs.text:
These options matter when the plugin needs to run R (record mode, or auto with a missing snapshot):

  • --r-snapshot-rscript=PATH / r_snapshot_rscript: which Rscript to run
  • --r-snapshot-cwd=PATH / r_snapshot_cwd: working directory for R
  • --r-snapshot-env=KEY=VALUE / r_snapshot_env: environment overrides (repeatable)
  • --r-snapshot-timeout=SECONDS / r_snapshot_timeout: per-chunk timeout
  • --r-snapshot-encoding=ENC / r_snapshot_encoding: snapshot file encoding

See docs/articles/configuration.md for the full reference and precedence rules.

docs.title: Markers (optional)
docs.text:
You can declare snapshot dependencies at the test boundary:

import pytest

@pytest.mark.r_snapshot("summary")
def test_summary_matches_r(r_snapshot):
    r_snapshot.assert_match_text(my_python_summary(...), name="summary")

Or use the decorator alias:

from pytest_r_snapshot import r_snapshot

@r_snapshot("summary")
def test_summary_matches_r(r_snapshot):
    ...

The marker is repeatable. It is used for better errors (for example, a test declares snapshot X but no chunk X exists).

docs.title: Normalizing output
docs.text:
The normalize= hook lets you apply domain-specific normalization to both expected and actual text before comparison.

Example: normalize newlines and strip trailing whitespace:

from pytest_r_snapshot import normalize_newlines, strip_trailing_whitespace

def normalize(text: str) -> str:
    return strip_trailing_whitespace(normalize_newlines(text))

def test_output(r_snapshot):
    ...
    r_snapshot.assert_match_text(actual, name="out", normalize=normalize)

docs.title: Reading and recording explicitly
docs.text:
• r_snapshot.read_text(name="...") reads a snapshot file (fails if missing).
  • r_snapshot.record_text(name="...") runs the labelled R chunk and writes the snapshot file.
  • r_snapshot.path_for(name="...") returns the snapshot path.

docs.title: Providing R code explicitly
docs.text:
For advanced use cases, you can pass R code directly via code=... to record_text() or assert_match_text():

  • If code contains fenced chunks, the chunk with label name is used.
  • Otherwise code is treated as the raw R body to execute.

docs.title: RChunk dataclass
docs.text: A labelled R chunk extracted from a Python source file.

docs.title: parse_r_chunks(path)
docs.text: Parse labelled R fenced chunks from a Python source file.

docs.title: parse_r_chunks_from_text(source, *, path)
docs.text: Parse labelled R fenced chunks from source text.

docs.title: ChunkLabelMissingError
docs.text:
Bases: ChunkParseError

Raised when an R fenced chunk is missing a required label.

docs.title: ChunkNotFoundError
docs.text:
Bases: RSnapshotError

Raised when an expected R chunk label cannot be found.

docs.title: ChunkParseError
docs.text:
Bases: RSnapshotError

Raised when parsing R chunks from a source file fails.

docs.title: DuplicateChunkLabelError
docs.text:
Bases: ChunkParseError

Raised when a Python file contains duplicate R chunk labels.

docs.title: RExecutionError
docs.text:
Bases: RSnapshotError

Raised when an R chunk execution fails.

docs.title: RSnapshotError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Base exception for pytest-r-snapshot.

docs.title: RscriptNotFoundError
docs.text:
Bases: RSnapshotError

Raised when the configured Rscript executable cannot be found.

docs.title: SnapshotNameError
docs.text:
Bases: RSnapshotError

Raised when a snapshot name is invalid for use as a filename.

docs.title: SnapshotNotFoundError
docs.text:
Bases: RSnapshotError

Raised when a snapshot file is missing in replay mode.

docs.title: UnclosedChunkError
docs.text:
Bases: ChunkParseError

Raised when an R fenced chunk start fence has no matching end fence.

docs.title: normalize_newlines(text)
docs.text:
Normalize line endings to \n.

Snapshot files are stored and compared using Unix newlines for determinism.

docs.title: strip_trailing_whitespace(text)
docs.text: Strip trailing spaces and tabs from each line.

docs.title: r_snapshot_effective_settings(pytestconfig, r_snapshot_settings)
docs.text: Effective settings after applying CLI overrides.

docs.title: r_snapshot_runner(pytestconfig, r_snapshot_effective_settings)
docs.text: Runner used to execute R code (overridable for tests).

docs.title: r_snapshot_settings(pytestconfig)
docs.text:
Session-scoped settings hook for configuring r-snapshot.

Users may override this fixture in conftest.py to provide custom defaults. CLI options still take precedence over this fixture.

docs.title: RRunner
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Execute R code and return captured stdout.

docs.title: run(code)
docs.text: Run R code and return captured output as text.

docs.title: SubprocessRRunner
docs.text: Execute R code via Rscript using :mod:subprocess.

docs.title: RSnapshotSettings dataclass
docs.text: Configuration for the r-snapshot plugin.

docs.title: SnapshotMode
docs.text:
Bases: str, Enum

Whether to replay existing snapshots or record new ones.

docs.title: parse(value) classmethod
docs.text: Parse a snapshot mode value from config/CLI.

docs.title: parse_env_assignments(values)
docs.text: Parse KEY=VALUE assignments into a dict.

docs.title: RSnapshot
docs.text:
Snapshot testing against reference outputs recorded from R.

This object is provided by the r_snapshot fixture. In replay mode, it reads snapshot files and compares them to Python-generated outputs. In record/auto modes, it executes the labelled R chunk and writes the snapshot file.

docs.title: assert_match_text(actual, *, name, ext='.txt', normalize=None, code=None)
docs.text: Assert that actual matches the expected snapshot content.

docs.title: path_for(name, *, ext='.txt')
docs.text: Return the on-disk path for a snapshot.

docs.title: read_text(name, *, ext='.txt')
docs.text: Read a text snapshot from disk.

docs.title: record_text(name, *, ext='.txt', code=None)
docs.text: Execute the R code for name and write its output as a snapshot.

docs.title: RSnapshot
docs.text:
Snapshot testing against reference outputs recorded from R.

This object is provided by the r_snapshot fixture. In replay mode, it reads snapshot files and compares them to Python-generated outputs. In record/auto modes, it executes the labelled R chunk and writes the snapshot file.

docs.title: assert_match_text(actual, *, name, ext='.txt', normalize=None, code=None)
docs.text: Assert that actual matches the expected snapshot content.

docs.title: path_for(name, *, ext='.txt')
docs.text: Return the on-disk path for a snapshot.

docs.title: read_text(name, *, ext='.txt')
docs.text: Read a text snapshot from disk.

docs.title: record_text(name, *, ext='.txt', code=None)
docs.text: Execute the R code for name and write its output as a snapshot.

docs.title: RSnapshotSettings dataclass
docs.text: Configuration for the r-snapshot plugin.

docs.title: SnapshotMode
docs.text:
Bases: str, Enum

Whether to replay existing snapshots or record new ones.

docs.title: parse(value) classmethod
docs.text: Parse a snapshot mode value from config/CLI.

docs.title: normalize_newlines(text)
docs.text:
Normalize line endings to \n.

Snapshot files are stored and compared using Unix newlines for determinism.

docs.title: r_snapshot(name, *, ext='.txt')
docs.text:
Decorator alias for :func:pytest.mark.r_snapshot.

This is a small convenience wrapper around the plugin's marker.

docs.title: strip_trailing_whitespace(text)
docs.text: Strip trailing spaces and tabs from each line.
